title: Application Support Engineer
description:
Job Description

·      The Application Support Engineer – Monitoring is responsible for ensuring the stability, availability, and performance of enterprise applications and infrastructure through proactive monitoring and rapid incident response. 

·      This role operates as the first line of defense against system disruptions, identifying issues early, initiating escalations, and supporting service continuity in a 24/7 operational environment.

·       The engineer collaborates closely with internal technical teams and external stakeholders to maintain SLA compliance and minimize business impact.

Key Responsibilities 

·      System Monitoring & Incident Response Continuously monitor enterprise applications, infrastructure components, and scheduled batch jobs using monitoring tools such as eGmon, Dynatrace, SAP HANA Cockpit, and batch job monitoring tools. 

·      Detect, analyze, and respond to system alerts, performance degradation, and batch failures in a timely manner.

·       Perform initial triage, validate incidents, and determine severity based on business impact.

·       Initiate and manage incident tickets in ITSM tools (e.g., ServiceNow) following defined operational procedures.

·       Coordination & Communication Escalate high-impact or critical incidents to relevant stakeholders, including project teams and leadership, as per escalation matrices. 

·      Provide clear, concise, and timely communication during incidents, including status updates and handovers across shifts.

·       Coordinate with application, infrastructure, database, and batch scheduling teams to support incident resolution.

·       Operational Support Support daily operational activities, including health checks, monitoring dashboards, and alert reviews.

·       Ensure adherence to SLAs, operational runbooks, and incident management processes.

·       Participate in shift-based operations, including nights, weekends, and holidays, as required in a 24/7 support model. 

Required Skills & Competencies 

·      Ability to work independently and remain calm under pressure during critical incidents. 

·      Strong analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills. 

·      Effective verbal and written communication skills for technical and non-technical stakeholders.

·       Willingness and readiness to learn and use ITSM tools (e.g., ServiceNow) and monitoring platforms such as Dynatrace, eGmon, and batch job monitoring tools. 

·      Preferred Qualifications Experience in application or infrastructure monitoring within an enterprise or managed services environment.

·       Familiarity with batch processing, job scheduling, and production support operations. Exposure to SAP landscapes (e.g., SAP HANA) and enterprise monitoring tools is an advantage.

·       Understanding of ITIL-based incident and escalation processes.

  • Work Environment 24/7 shift-based operational support environment High-availability, mission-critical enterprise systems Fast-paced setting requiring proactive monitoring and rapid response.
